And the other one was, send me into any city, we'll pick a homeless person off the street or somebody out of the unemployment office line, and I'll take them, and 48 hours later they'll own real estate and have positive cashflow and have an income and yada yada yada, right? It was a dramatic demonstration, right? It was undoubtedly just as rigged as the fire or walk. I don't know that for a fact, by the way. I'm only speculating. If it wasn't, he's not half as smart as I have always given him credit for. Because to go do that and not have it rigged, not real bright. But it is a dramatic demonstration, right? What has put Disney in the business training business, the dramatic demonstration of what happens at Disney, their whole experience is a dramatic demonstration. I just went, first time I went, I went a couple of weeks ago when I was doing Hutter's boot camp. So I'm at his boot camp, so I flew up to High Point University for the first time. And how many of you have been to High Point where Cabana is, okay, not enough of you. You got to go. It's like not going to Disney. You got to go. So put it on your list. And I don't mean your bucket list. You got to go like soon, right? Especially if your business has a component part where people somehow come to you. It's not as important to see if it doesn't have that. But if your business has a component part where they come to you, and I don't care if it's a little tiny office or it's something big, or it's an event, or whatever. If they come to you, this is one you got to go see. We may organize a seminar and a trip or something next year, as a matter of fact. But because it is, if Disney did college, this would be it. And it is from beginning to end, it is its own dramatic demonstration. And as a result, almost no selling has to be done. And an actual rational connection between price and deliverable is gone. For the right person, the right parent, the right parent sees this demonstration and will kill. To get their kid in there and not him go anyplace else. Some more down-to-earth examples, right? Joe Polish and I, he invented I embellished. The carpet audit, right? So the guy comes and doesn't just clean the carpets. He does this big elaborate audit process, diagnostic process. Before he does it, right? So what really is diagnosis? It's a form of dramatic demonstration. We got chiropractors. Where are my chiropractors? So there's three of you. Who does the best report of findings? Who thinks they do a really good report of findings? Nobody's courageous, huh? We have them and nobody raises their hand. See, if that's right, so report of findings. How many of you have been to a chiropractor office? A bunch of you. How many go now? Chiropractor treatment. That's not a bad retention rate. Well, you guys all saw it. The question is, did you see it done well? See, that's the question. Because the diagnosis is that, because you can't tell, right? You don't know. If he could be the best chiropractor in the world, he could be the worst chiropractor in the world. How do you know? How can you tell? In reality, you can't even tell necessarily when he treats you. You can make a judgment if he's really awful, like you were half crippled before, you're completely crippled now. You'll make an assessment about that, right? Not only is the pain worse afterwards, but three days later it's still worse. You'll make an assessment about that. But generally speaking, you'll never know. On a one to ten, is he a ten, an eight, a seven? You'll never know. How will you ever know? You can't know. And you certainly can't know when you're buying, and he can't really demonstrate the thing. I mean, he can give you an adjustment, I guess. But since you don't know what that's supposed to be,
